title: Aluminum price falls on US-Iran deal but supply normalization to take time: analysts
summary: Aluminum prices declined following the US-Iran deal, though analysts note that supply normalization will take time to fully materialize in the market. The report highlights accelerating shifts in aluminum recycling from a green initiative to a necessity.

title: The Aluminum Shock Hitting the Global Economy

title: Aluminum closed in on prices not seen since 2022 following Iranian attacks

title: AI data centers' massive demand for aluminum is crushing the US aluminum industry
summary: A new aluminum smelter in Oklahoma with a capacity of about 600,000 tons per year is nearly doubling current US domestic production, which currently accounts for less than 1% of global output. The U.S. government must intervene to increase domestic production and reduce reliance on foreign suppliers, as even at full capacity, U.S. smelters could only satisfy about a third of domestic demand.

title: America's big new aluminum smelter is still waiting on a power deal
summary: America's new aluminum smelter is still waiting on a power deal, with companies expecting to start construction in late 2026 and begin producing metal by the end of the decade. Finalizing the power agreement remains a critical step before construction can commence.

title: New filter captures and recycles aluminum from manufacturing waste
summary: MIT engineers developed a nanofiltration process that captures over 99% of aluminum ions from hazardous manufacturing waste, enabling upcycling and reducing effluent pollution. The membrane selectively retains aluminum while allowing sodium and other cations to pass through, as demonstrated in lab-scale experiments.

title: Aluminum: Driving Efficiency and Sustainability Across All Vehicle Segments

title: United States Steel Production

title: Latest developments in steelmaking capacity and outlook until 2026
summary: World crude steelmaking capacity in 2023 is estimated at 2,432 million tonnes, exceeding global production by 543 million tonnes. Between 2024 and 2026, an additional 68.3 million tonnes of capacity is expected to come on stream, with gross additions potentially reaching 157.0 million tonnes.

title: The Boom in New Steel Mills Is Outpacing Demand

Steel manufacturers are constructing new facilities to contribute millions of tons to the domestic market, though demand must sustain this growth.

title: Industry Data

title: 3 innovations in green steel
summary: This article highlights Boston Metal, a Bill Gates-backed startup developing a CO2-free steel process using molten oxide electrolysis, which recently raised $50 million. title: Smelting Barriers: The Innovators Forging a New Future for Iron and Steel
summary: The report details emerging technologies like direct electrification and hydrogen injection that are heating up the steel market, with Northern Sweden set to produce the first commercial near-zero emissions steel. title: Iron and Steel Technology Roadmap
summary: This roadmap outlines critical pathways for the steel industry to cut emissions by at least 50% by 2050, including hydrogen, carbon capture, bioenergy, and direct electrification. It recommends establishing long-term CO2 reduction signals, creating markets for near-zero emissions steel, and accelerating material efficiency to support the global transition.

title: Green transformation in the iron and steel industry in India
summary: New technologies are being developed in India to significantly cut carbon emissions in the iron and steel sector, with major steel companies actively pursuing various technology avenues and seeking relevant partners. The article underscores the active involvement of India's steel majors in research and development efforts to drive green transformation.

title: Low-Carbon Production of Iron & Steel: Technology Options, Economic Assessment and Policy
summary: This paper reviews global iron and steel production and assesses decarbonization technologies such as hydrogen injection, solid biomass substitution, and zero-carbon electricity. It finds that replacing fossil fuel injection with biofuels can reduce greenhouse gas emissions by up to 25%, with potential reductions of up to 58% if the biomass is carbon neutral.

title: Italy's Tenaris posts 2% rise in quarterly net sales

title: Prysmian shares rise as Italian cable maker seen as winner amid US copper tariffs

title: Prysmian Group - Wikipedia
summary: Prysmian S.p.A. is a multinational company headquartered in Milan, Italy, specializing in electrical cables for energy and telecom sectors and optical fibres[4]. The company is recognized as a leading player in the copper wire and cable market operating in over 50 countries[4][8].

title: Copper Cables - Prysmian
summary: Prysmian produces a range of copper telecoms cables designed for special applications and environments, particularly those requiring fire-resistance characteristics[9]. These cables support the company's role as the world leader in cable manufacturing and telecom solutions[7][9].
